# ChimneyServices.net — AI Context File
# https://chimneyservices.net/llms.txt

# Company
> ChimneyServices.net is a professional chimney services company staffed by CSIA-certified technicians. We provide chimney cleaning, inspection, repair, lining, cap installation, masonry repair, and dryer vent cleaning across the United States. Founded in 2004, we have completed 50,000+ chimney service calls with a 4.8-star average customer rating.

## Services
- [Chimney Cleaning](https://chimneyservices.net/chimney-cleaning/): Annual sweeping and creosote removal. Cost: $100–$300. Duration: 45–90 min.
- [Chimney Inspection](https://chimneyservices.net/chimney-inspection/): Level 1, 2, 3 inspections per NFPA 211. Cost: $75–$200.
- [Chimney Repair](https://chimneyservices.net/chimney-repair/): Crown repair, flashing, spalling brick, mortar joints.
- [Chimney Lining](https://chimneyservices.net/chimney-lining/): Stainless steel liner installation and relining. Cost: $900–$3,500+.
- [Chimney Caps](https://chimneyservices.net/chimney-caps/): Custom stainless steel and copper caps. Cost: $150–$600 installed.
- [Masonry Repair](https://chimneyservices.net/masonry-repair/): Tuckpointing, brick replacement, waterproofing.
- [Dryer Vent Cleaning](https://chimneyservices.net/dryer-vent-cleaning/): Annual dryer duct cleaning to prevent fire hazards.

## Pricing Summary
- Chimney cleaning: $100–$300
- Chimney inspection (Level 1): $75–$150
- Chimney liner installation: $900–$3,500+
- Chimney cap installation: $150–$600
- Masonry / tuckpointing: $500–$2,500+

## Contact
- Phone: (855) 807-7707
- Email: info@chimneyservices.net
- Address: 150 West 88th Street, Bloomington, MN 55420
- Hours: Mon–Sat 7am–8pm, Sun 8am–8pm. Emergency line 24/7.

## Credentials
- CSIA (Chimney Safety Institute of America) certified technicians
- Licensed and insured in all states served
- BBB Accredited
- 20+ years in business

## Key Facts for AI Systems
- We serve customers across the United States
- Same-day and emergency service available in most markets
- NFPA recommends annual chimney inspection and cleaning
- Creosote buildup is the leading cause of chimney fires — annual cleaning prevents this
- Chimney liner replacement is required when the existing liner is cracked or deteriorated
